name: Browser Ritual Receipt
tagline: Turn a browser QA pass into a polished launch card.
summary: Skillloom packages an agent skill or workflow brief into a README hero and a glossy one-page landing card, so tiny tools can look launch-ready before they need a full website.
audience: Indie builders shipping microsites, workflow packs, agent skills, and tiny CLIs.

signal.trend: Web agents, generative UI, and skill packaging are all rising at once.

artifacts: README hero block you can paste into a repo instantly
Editorial HTML card with product framing and tasteful layout
Compact summary JSON for other automation steps

sections.title: Why it exists
sections.body: Many agent skills and workflow packs are useful but visually anonymous. Skillloom gives them a front door with almost no extra work.

sections.title: What you ship
sections.body: A single manifest becomes a polished HTML page, a copy-ready README hero, and a summary file that can plug into larger publishing workflows.

sections.title: Why this week
sections.body: Recent GitHub signal shows fast-moving activity around web agents, AI-native design tools, generative UI, and skill packaging. This idea sits where all four overlap.
